summary: Heavily obfuscated code in main entry warrants manual review
details: The package's main entry src/index.js contains heavily obfuscated JavaScript using string-array shift/rotate patterns (e.g., while(!![]), ['shift'](), hex-indexed array accesses) characteristic of obfuscator.io-style packing. Obfuscation alone is not proof of malicious intent — some authors obfuscate to protect proprietary logic — but it prevents straightforward verification of behavior and is a common cover for credential theft, exfiltration, or remote-code-loading. No specific exfiltration endpoint, credential read path, or install-time lifecycle hook has been confirmed in the available evidence, so the disposition is human review rather than block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
